dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7929 IH: Firefighter Lithium-ion Awareness and Readiness Enhancement Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Titus introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Transportation to issue certain regulations with respect to the safe transportation of lithium-ion cells or batteries, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Firefighter Lithium-ion Awareness and Readiness Enhancement Act or the FLARE Act.

section.header: Increasing access to thermal runaway suppression tools
section.text: Section 33 of the Federal Fire Prevention and Control Act of 1974 (15 U.S.C. 2229) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (7) and (8) as paragraphs (8) and (9), respectively;
by inserting after paragraph (6) the following:

paragraph.text: The term lithium-ion cell or battery—

subparagraph.text: means a rechargeable electrochemical cell or battery in which the positive and negative electrodes are both intercalation compounds constructed with no metallic lithium in either electrode; but
does not include a cell or battery described in subsections (c) and (g)(2) of section 173.185 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations.
by inserting after paragraph (9) (as so redesignated) the following:

paragraph.text: The term thermal runaway means an uncontrolled increase of cell temperature caused by exothermic reactions inside cells and batteries, including lithium-ion cells or batteries.

subparagraph.text: in clause (iv) by striking or at the end;
in clause (v) by striking the period at the end and inserting , including lithium-ion cells or batteries; or; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: suppressing fires resulting from thermal runaway.

subparagraph.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (E) as subparagraph (F); and
by inserting after subparagraph (D) the following:
To fund programs to suppress fires resulting from thermal runaway.
